title: Phyllanthus stipulatus
taxonomy: Phyllanthus stipulatus is classified in the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Phyllanthus family. This family is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large order of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which includes most flowering plants. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the clade Angiosperms, which comprises all flowering plants. The clade Angiosperms is part of the larger clade Spermatophyta, which includes all plants that produce seeds.

intro: Phyllanthus stipulatus, also known as 'Dhava', is a small shrub or tree native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including liver and kidney disorders, as well as fever, inflammation, and infections, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of P. stipulatus include flavonoids, saponins, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, P. stipulatus is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to support liver and kidney function, as well as to alleviate symptoms of fever and inflammation.
